edges.source: Impaired CNP-NPR-B-cGMP Signaling in Growth Plate

edges.target: Impaired CNP-NPR-B-cGMP Signaling in Growth Plate

nodes.description: Shortening predominantly affects the middle and distal segments of the upper and lower limbs, with forearms, lower legs, hands, and feet more severely affected than proximal segments.

evidence.reference_title: Acromesomelic dysplasia-Maroteaux type, nine patients with two novel NPR2 variants.
evidence.snippet: It is a rare type of dwarfism characterized by shortening of the middle and distal segments of the limbs with spondylar dysplasia.

evidence.reference_title: Novel variants in natriuretic peptide receptor 2 in unrelated patients with acromesomelic dysplasia type Maroteaux.
evidence.snippet: Acromesomelic dysplasia type Maroteaux (AMDM) is characterized by extreme shortening of the forelimbs and disproportionate short stature.

nodes.description: Bowing of the long bones of the extremities may be seen radiographically in AMDM, reflecting abnormal endochondral growth.

evidence.snippet: HP:0006487 | Bowing of the long bones | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Short and broad metacarpals, metatarsals, and phalanges with characteristic cone-shaped epiphyses. This is a cardinal feature visible on radiographic examination.

evidence.reference_title: A novel NPR2 mutation (p.Arg388Gln) in a patient with acromesomelic dysplasia, type Maroteaux.
evidence.snippet: She had acromesomelic shortening of limbs and severe brachydactyly.

evidence.reference_title: A mild case of acromesomelic dysplasia, type Maroteaux with novel natriuretic peptide receptor B (NPR2) variants.
evidence.snippet: Clinically he has mild mesomelic shortening, more evident in his upper limbs, and brachydactyly

nodes.description: Cone-shaped epiphyses of the phalanges are a characteristic radiographic finding; premature fusion has been described in some affected phalanges.

evidence.reference_title: A mild case of acromesomelic dysplasia, type Maroteaux with novel natriuretic peptide receptor B (NPR2) variants.
evidence.snippet: DP left hand (A) with broadening and shortening of the bones, and significant coned appearance of the epiphyses

nodes.description: A depressed nasal bridge is part of the mild craniofacial features seen in AMDM, contributing to the characteristic facial appearance.

evidence.snippet: HP:0005280 | Depressed nasal bridge | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Prominent forehead and frontal bossing are part of the characteristic craniofacial phenotype of AMDM, though craniofacial involvement is mild compared to limb shortening.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002007 | Frontal bossing | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Increased lumbar lordosis is frequently observed in AMDM, often accompanying kyphosis and contributing to the overall spinal abnormality.

evidence.snippet: HP:0003307 | Hyperlordosis | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: NPR-B is a transmembrane guanylyl cyclase receptor that converts GTP to cGMP upon binding its ligand CNP. Biallelic loss-of-function mutations in NPR2 eliminate cGMP production in growth plate chondrocytes, disrupting downstream signaling that normally promotes chondrocyte proliferation and hypertrophy.
nodes.id: Impaired CNP-NPR-B-cGMP Signaling in Growth Plate

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the transmembrane natriuretic peptide receptor NPR-B impair skeletal growth and cause acromesomelic dysplasia, type Maroteaux.
evidence.snippet: We conclude that, although NPR-B is expressed in a number of tissues, its major role is in the regulation of skeletal growth.

evidence.reference_title: The cn/cn dwarf mouse. Histomorphometric, ultrastructural, and radiographic study in mutants corresponding to human acromesomelic dysplasia Maroteaux type (AMDM).
evidence.snippet: The main physeal abnormality was a markedly shortened hypertrophic zone

evidence.reference_title: A novel loss-of-function mutation in Npr2 clarifies primary role in female reproduction and reveals a potential therapy for acromesomelic dysplasia, Maroteaux type.
evidence.snippet: We determined that the loss of Npr2 causes a reduction in the hypertrophic and proliferative zones of the growth plate, but mineralization of skeletal elements is normal.

nodes.description: Increased joint laxity and hypermobility may be seen in AMDM, potentially related to abnormal peri-articular connective tissue development.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001382 | Joint hypermobility | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Increased thoracic curvature (kyphosis) is frequently observed in AMDM and may be combined with lordotic changes of the lumbar spine.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002808 | Kyphosis | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Failure of normal widening of the lumbar interpedicular distance can be seen on spinal radiographs, reflecting axial skeletal involvement.

evidence.reference_title: A mild case of acromesomelic dysplasia, type Maroteaux with novel natriuretic peptide receptor B (NPR2) variants.
evidence.snippet: failure of widening of the lumbar interpedicular distances with some shortening of the pedicles.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the transmembrane natriuretic peptide receptor NPR-B impair skeletal growth and cause acromesomelic dysplasia, type Maroteaux.
evidence.snippet: Three missense mutations were tested in a functional assay and were found to have markedly deficient guanylyl cyclase activity.

evidence.reference_title: A novel NPR2 mutation (p.Arg388Gln) in a patient with acromesomelic dysplasia, type Maroteaux.
evidence.snippet: Cells expressing HA-R388Q-NPRB showed negligible cGMP responses to C-type natriuretic peptide (CNP) stimulation, indicating that the mutation led to severe loss-of-function.

evidence.reference_title: Novel variants in natriuretic peptide receptor 2 in unrelated patients with acromesomelic dysplasia type Maroteaux.
evidence.snippet: Several homozygous inactivating mutations in NPR2 have been identified in different AMDM patients. We report five novel variants in affected individuals in four different families.

nodes.description: Ovoid vertebral body shape is a characteristic radiographic finding in AMDM reflecting mild axial endochondral bone growth impairment.

evidence.snippet: HP:0003300 | Ovoid vertebral bodies | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Acromesomelic dysplasia-Maroteaux type, nine patients with two novel NPR2 variants.
evidence.snippet: It is a rare type of dwarfism characterized by shortening of the middle and distal segments of the limbs with spondylar dysplasia.

nodes.description: Mild vertebral body flattening with wedging (shorter posterior than anterior height) is present. Vertebral changes are milder than limb involvement.

evidence.reference_title: A novel NPR2 mutation (p.Arg388Gln) in a patient with acromesomelic dysplasia, type Maroteaux.
evidence.snippet: Her vertebral bodies were mildly flattened

evidence.reference_title: A mild case of acromesomelic dysplasia, type Maroteaux with novel natriuretic peptide receptor B (NPR2) variants.
evidence.snippet: Pear shaped vertebral bodies with a shorter posterior versus anterior height

nodes.description: Mid-diaphyseal bowing of the radius may be present, reflecting abnormal endochondral growth of the forearm bones.

evidence.reference_title: A mild case of acromesomelic dysplasia, type Maroteaux with novel natriuretic peptide receptor B (NPR2) variants.
evidence.snippet: Radial bowing, flared metaphysis and occasional hypoplasia of the distal radius

nodes.description: Impaired chondrocyte hypertrophy and proliferation in the growth plate result in shortened endochondral bones, affecting limbs (particularly middle and distal segments) and vertebral bodies. Normal ultrastructure of individual chondrocytes indicates the defect is in growth plate organization rather than intrinsic cell structure.

evidence.reference_title: The cn/cn dwarf mouse. Histomorphometric, ultrastructural, and radiographic study in mutants corresponding to human acromesomelic dysplasia Maroteaux type (AMDM).
evidence.snippet: Cn/cn mice were two thirds the size of the cn/+.

evidence.reference_title: The cn/cn dwarf mouse. Histomorphometric, ultrastructural, and radiographic study in mutants corresponding to human acromesomelic dysplasia Maroteaux type (AMDM).
evidence.snippet: The disorder causes a shortened physeal hypertrophic zone but normal ultrastructure of cn/cn chondrocytes points to abnormality primarily affecting the hypertrophic zone rather than a structural cell or matrix synthesis problem.

nodes.description: Lateral curvature of the spine is a frequent spinal complication in AMDM, reflecting the mild axial skeletal involvement secondary to vertebral changes.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002650 | Scoliosis | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Adult height is typically below 120 cm with disproportionate involvement of the limbs relative to the trunk. Growth may appear normal at birth but decreased growth becomes evident in the first two years of life.

evidence.reference_title: A mild case of acromesomelic dysplasia, type Maroteaux with novel natriuretic peptide receptor B (NPR2) variants.
evidence.snippet: Postnatal skeletal growth is reduced and final height is usually below 120 cm

evidence.reference_title: Novel Loss-of-Function Mutations in NPR2 Cause Acromesomelic Dysplasia, Maroteaux Type.
evidence.snippet: Acromesomelic dysplasia, Maroteaux type (AMDM) is a rare skeletal dysplasia characterized by severe disproportionate short stature, short hands and feet, normal intelligence, and facial dysmorphism.

evidence.reference_title: A novel NPR2 mutation (p.Arg388Gln) in a patient with acromesomelic dysplasia, type Maroteaux.
evidence.snippet: Her height was 119.0 cm (-7.4 SD) and weight 35 kg (-2.3 SD).

nodes.description: The feet are characteristically short and broad, paralleling the distal limb shortening seen in the hands.

evidence.reference_title: Novel Loss-of-Function Mutations in NPR2 Cause Acromesomelic Dysplasia, Maroteaux Type.
evidence.snippet: Acromesomelic dysplasia, Maroteaux type (AMDM) is a rare skeletal dysplasia characterized by severe disproportionate short stature, short hands and feet, normal intelligence, and facial dysmorphism.

evidence.reference_title: A mild case of acromesomelic dysplasia, type Maroteaux with novel natriuretic peptide receptor B (NPR2) variants.
evidence.snippet: Photographs showing brachydactyly with broad fingers (A) and short, broad toes and feet (B).

nodes.description: Mesomelic shortening of the forearms is a prominent feature, reflecting impaired longitudinal growth of the radius and ulna.

evidence.reference_title: Novel variants in natriuretic peptide receptor 2 in unrelated patients with acromesomelic dysplasia type Maroteaux.
evidence.snippet: Acromesomelic dysplasia type Maroteaux (AMDM) is characterized by extreme shortening of the forelimbs and disproportionate short stature.

nodes.description: The metacarpals are short and broad, contributing to the characteristic hand appearance in AMDM.

evidence.reference_title: A novel NPR2 mutation (p.Arg388Gln) in a patient with acromesomelic dysplasia, type Maroteaux.
evidence.snippet: Radiological examination showed that her metacarpals and phalanges were short and wide
